authors: The community (Roll20,Reddit,Pinterest,ecc.) if with a link i put in the description of monster

legendaryGroup.name: p4535992StradhMixLairActionsTheRealDevilStrahd
lairActions: While Strahd is in Castle Ravenloft, he can take lair actions as long as he isn’t incapacitated. On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), Strahd can take one of the following lair action options, or forgo using any of them in that round: Until initiative count 20 of the next round, Strahd can pass through solid walls, doors, ceilings, and floors as if they weren’t there.

items: Strahd targets any number of doors and windows that he can see, causing each one to either open or close as he wishes. Closed doors can be magically locked (needing a successful DC 20 Strength check to force open) until Strahd chooses to end the effect, or until Strahd uses this lair action again.

Strahd summons the angry spirit of one who has died in the castle. The apparition appears next to a hostile creature that Strahd can see, makes an attack against that creature, and then disappears. The apparition has the statistics of a specter.

Strahd targets one Medium or smaller creature that casts a shadow. The target’s shadow must be visible to Strahd and within 30 feet of him. If the target fails a DC 22 Charisma saving throw, its shadow detaches from it and becomes a shadow that obeys Strahd’s commands, acting on initiative count 20. A greater restoration spell or a remove curse spell cast on the target restores its natural shadow, but only if its undead shadow has been destroyed.

A shadowy and deathly mist rises from the ground in a 120-foot radius centered on Strahd, and the mist spreads around corners. This area is lightly obscured. Living creatures within the mist are vulnerable to necrotic damage. Undead within the mist have resistance to radiant damage. Light within the mist can be no brighter than dim unless created by a spell of 5th level or higher, or equivalent magic. The mist lasts for 1 minute, but a strong wind can disperse it, and sunlight burns the mist away instantly and clears the area such light touches.

The psychic impressions of the ancient one’s innumerable victims rises in the area for 1 minute. For this time, each creature hostile to and within 120 feet of the ancient one is overwhelmed by the voices of the dead and distracted by disturbing sensory input. Such a creature is deafened, and it has disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks. At the end of each of its turns, a creature can make a DC 22 Wisdom saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. If this saving throw fails by 5 or more, the affected creature can’t maintain concentration until the start of its next turn

trait.name: En Garde!
entries: While Ireena has a one-handed Light or Finesse weapon in her main hand, she has advantage on any Dexterity save she makes. She loses this advantage if her weapons are sheathed, she is disarmed, or otherwise not wielding her weapon.

trait.name: Swagger
entries: Swagger Ireena gains an expression of her swashbuckling skill, the points in this pool may be expended to perform special maneuvers or class features. At the end of any encounter, she regain one point of swagger. After a short rest, the swagger pool is completely restored.
She may spend a maximum of 3 points of swagger on any single turn.
If she runs out of swagger pool points, she gains advantage on any Acrobatics check she might make.
The following maneuvers are available to her.
Note: For maneuvers that require a saving throw, the DC is equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + Ireena's Charisma modifier(DC15) unless stated otherwise.

- Cheap Shot: When attempting to attack a creature, Ireena may spend two points of swagger in order to perform a cheap shot. If her attack is successful, choose one of the following tricks: Ear Slap (deafen), Eye Poke (blind), Foot Stomp (immobilize), Low Blow (stun, male humanoid only). Status effects from this maneuver last a single round and are only effective if the target is not immune to critical hits and has the appropriate body parts. Once you use this maneuver against a creature, you must complete a short or long rest before you can attempt to use it against that same creature again.

- Disarm: When Ireena hits a creature with a weapon attack, she can spend one point of swagger to attempt to disarm the target, forcing it to drop one item of her choice that it is holding. The target is allowed a Strength save. On a failed save, it drops the object she chooses. The object lands on any unoccupied or unobstructed square within ten feet of the target.

- Feint attack: Ireena can spend two points of swagger and use a bonus action on her turn to feint attack, choosing one creature within 5 feet of her as her target. She has advantage on any attack roll she makes against that creature with her next Attack action.

- Manchette: On a successful attack, Ireena may spend two points of swagger to target the opponent's weapon arm. Cutting deep into the muscle and severing its attachment to the bone, the target's Strength modifier damage with that arm is halved until the wound is mended. This includes damage from twohanded weapons but for dual-wielders, damage from the other arm is not affected.

- Play Dead: After receiving a damaging hit, Ireena may spend one point of swagger to use her reaction to channel her inner actor and pretend to die a vivid and melodramatic death. Falling to the ground and considered {@condition prone}. Upon doing so, she makes a Deception or Performance check against her attacker's Insight if successful, they assume she has been brutally slain, and will react accordingly.

- Rally: On her turn, Ireena can use a bonus action and spend one point of swagger to bolster the resolve of one of her companions. When she does so, choose a friendly creature who can see or hear her. That creature gains a bonus on their next attack roll or save equal to her Charisma modifier.
- Riposte: When a creature misses Ireena with a melee attack, she can use her reaction and spend one point of swagger to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

- Taunt: When Ireena hits a creature with a weapon attack, she can spend one point of swagger to attempt to goad the target into attacking her. The target is allowed to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has disadvantage on all attack rolls against targets other than Ireena until the end of Ireena's next turn.

trait.name: Secret Identity
entries: Ireena has developed a costumed alter ego to move freely in barovia. All creatures that aren't aware of the disguise have disadvantage and a -3 penalty on any attempts to discern her true identity. This ability doesn't work with creatures that are familiar with Ireena If a nearby creature ever discerns her true identity, she can expend one point of swagger and make a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw, if successful the target no longer believes that Ireena and her secret identity are one and the same.

trait.name: A Fearsome Reputation
entries: Ireena's reputation precedes her, Peasants have heard that the Devil Strahd is closely tied to her and would never risk the wraith of Strahd to fall upon them. Ireena has advantage on intimidation and persuasion checks towards peasants and villagers that recognise her.

reaction.name: Parry
entries: Ireena adds 2 to her AC against one melee attack that would hit her. To do so, Ireena must be able to see the attacker and be wielding a melee weapon.

action.name: Heirloom Rapier +1
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8+2}) slashing damage.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4+3}) piercing damage in melee, or 5 ({@damage 1d4+3}) piercing damage at range.
{@link Ireena Kolyana|https://www.dmsguild.com/product/226076/Ireena-Kolyana-The-noble-with-the-Rapier}
She dresses in beautiful medieval gothic clothes that are comfortable to fight in. She carries a rapier at her side, sometimes a knife in her boots and a cross on her neck.
entries.by: Barovian Peasant's description when asked about Ireena.

entries.name: Description
entries: This document contains a CR5 Version of Ireena Kolyana. I felt that the book version was a bit lazy compared to the potention the character has. According to the book, Ireena is quite skilled in the use of a Rapier and a dynamic woman. I thought of making a better statblock for her while staying true on the descriptions. Ireena ,in my eyes, is shown to be very strong willed and stubborn, despite how much trouble it gets her into.Although she is charmed by Strahd, she realises her situation and is determined to reach safety. Despite her tough exterior, her caring nature shines through in her concern for her family. Sometimes she is a bit cold to the ones surrounding her but in reality she is a charming woman who can wield a weapon as good as she can stand up for herself in a verbal conflict.

entries.name: Abilities
entries: I have made custom Swashbuckler-ish mechanics which let Ireena utilise different abilities in order to assist the party ( and herself) in the best possible way that she can at every encounter. She might be a 'damsel in distress but she can handle herself quite good. She also has roleplaying mechanics like the secret identity and fearsome reputation that are really important for a character like her who is the daughter of a political figure and the victim of the lord of the land.

entries.name: How to use this ?
entries: Ireena should be played as a support character who knows that she is in danger but will not stand idle while others save her. She will do anything in her power to find an equilibrium between assisting the party and not becoming a hindrance/getting in much trouble.

monster.name: The Real Devil Strahd

resist.preNote: Damage Resistances of the Swamp Fane

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: If Strahd isn’t in running water or sunlight, he can use his bonus action to polymorph into a Tiny bat, a Medium or large wolf, or a Medium cloud of mist, or back into his true form. While in bat or wolf form, Strahd can’t speak. In bat form, his walking speed is 5 feet, and he has a flying speed of 40 feet. In wolf form, his walking speed is 40 feet. His statistics, other than size and speed are unchanged. Anything he is wearing transforms with him, but nothing he is carrying does. He reverts to his true form if he dies. While in mist form, Strand can’t take any actions, speak, or manipulate objects. He is weightless, has a flying speed of 60 feet, can hover, can enter a hostile creature’s space and stop there. In addition, if air can pass through a space, the mist can do so without squeezing, and he can’t pass through water. He has advantage on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws, and he is immune to all nonmagical damage, except the damage he takes from sunlight.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Strahd fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: Strahd regains 30 hit points at the start of his turn if he has at least 1 hit point and isn’t in running water or sunlight. If he takes radiant damage or damage from holy water, this trait doesn’t function at the start of his next turn

trait.name: Preternatural Speed
entries: Strahd can use a bonus action to take the Dash or Disengage action.

trait.name: Misty Escape
entries: When Strahd drops to 0 hit points outside his coffin, he transforms into a cloud of mist (as in the Shapechanger trait) instead of falling {@condition unconscious}, provided that he isn’t in running water or sunlight. If he can’t transform he is destroyed. While he has 0 hit points in mist form, he can’t revert to his vampire form, and he must reach his coffin within 2 hours or be destroyed. Once in his coffin, he reverts to his vampire form and is {@condition paralyzed} for 1 hour until he regains 1 hit point.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: Strahd’s attacks are considered magical for overcoming resistances.

trait.name: Rejunevation
entries: If Strahd is killed, he regains all his hit points at the next sunset unless his body was destroyed or his head was removed or his heart was pierced with a wooden stake and the stake is not removed, in which case it will take {@dice 1d10} year to rejuvenate at the location of his death.

trait.name: Unholy Persistence
entries: Strahd is immune to effects that turn undead unless the Icon of ravenkind was used to turn him.

trait.name: Monstrous Form
entries: If Strahd isn’t in sunlight and if he has fewer than half of his hit points remaining, he immediately polymorphs into a Large monstrous form for 10 minutes. His statistics, other than his size and speed, are unchanged. Anything he is wearing transforms with him, but nothing he is carrying does. He reverts to his true form if he dies or if there are no longer any active threats to hunt down. Strahd must rest for a full night after this transformation. While in monstrous form, Strahd has the following traits:
- Strahd gains an additional action on each of its turns. That action can be used only to take the Attack (one weapon attack only) or Dash action.
- Strahd grows batlike wings and gains a flying speed of 150 feet.
- Strahd has resistance to all damage except radiant damage.
- Strahd is under a blood frenzy. He has advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn’t have all its hit points and cannot use his shapechanger feature.
- Strahd can use the monstrous form once every 24 hours.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: Strahd can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without having to make an ability check.

trait.name: Vampire Weaknesses
entries: - Harmed by Running Water. He takes 10 acid damage if he ends his turn in running water.
- Stake to the Heart. If a piercing weapon made of wood is driven into his heart while he is {@condition incapacitated} in his coffin, he is {@condition paralyzed} until the stake is removed.
- Sunlight Hypersensitivity. While in sunlight, Strahd takes 20 radiant damage at the start of his turn, and he has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

trait.name: Blood Scent
entries: Strahd can sense living creatures that have blood or similar to blood vital fluids in a radius of 240 feet.

trait.name: Wilderness Fanes
entries: The Three Wilderness Fanes were once three sacred sites in Barovia, each guarded by one of the priestesses that are known today as the Three Hags of Barovia. When Strahd corrupted the land with dark rituals, the Fanes were corrupted. Each one gives a concrete and continual mechanical benefit to Strahd. Each priestess turned hag dwells in the fane prottecting it. If Strahd’s Links to the fanes is cut; he loses the benefits provided by that fane.

trait.name: Mountain Fane
entries: The most ancient of the Three Wilderness Fanes, located on Lysaga Hill. As a result of his connection to the mountains of Barovia, Strahd is surrounded by a field of force armor that grants him a +6 armor bonus to AC. Because it is made of force, this armor protects him against incorporeal attacks and it also applies when Strahd is in gaseous form. If Strahd’s connection to the Mountain Fane is severed, his Armor Class is reduced by 6 and his CR is reduced by 1.

trait.name: Swamp Fane
entries: The Swamp Fane is one of the Three Wilderness Fanes, located in Ivlis Marsh. It grants Strahd several elemental resistances. If Strahd’s connection to the Swamp Fane is severed, he loses resistance to Fire,Cold,Lightning,Thunder,Acid and his CR is reduced by 1.

trait.name: Forest Fane
entries: The Forest Fane is one of the Three Wilderness Fanes, located in a random place of any forest in Barovia. It grants Strahd continual nondetection and the abillity to eavesdrop and know the location of anyone who mentions his name. If Strahd’s connection to the Forest fane is severed, he loses this benefit and his CR is reduced by 1.

trait.name: Heart of Sorrow
entries: Any damage that Strahd takes is transferred to the Heart of Sorrow. If the heart absorbs damage that reduces it to O hit points, it is destroyed, and Strahd takes any leftover damage. The Heart of Sorrow has 100 hit points and is restored to that number of hit points each dawn, provided it has at least 1 hit point remaining. Strahd can, as a bonus action on his turn, break his link to the Heart of Sorrow so that it no longer absorbs damage dealt to him. Strahd can reestablish his link to the Heart of Sorrow as a bonus action on his turn, but only while in Castle Ravenloft.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Strahd is a 16nth-level spellcaster. His spellcasting ability is Intelligence (spell save {@dc 22}, +14 to hit with spell attacks). Strahd has the following spells prepared:

spells: {@spell Rain of Terror (Strahd's New spells)|p4535992Spells|rain of terror}
{@spell Strahd's baneful Attractor (Strahd's New spells)|p4535992Spells|strahd's baneful attractor}

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Strahd makes three attacks, any combination of longsword and unarmed strike or four unarmed strikes in Monstrous form.

action.name: Blacksteel longsword +3(Vampire Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}24 ({@damage 3d10 + 8}) slashing damage, plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Charm
entries: Strahd targets one creature he can see within 60 feet of him. If the target can see Strahd, the target must succeed on a {@dc 22} Wisdom saving throw against this magic or be {@condition charmed}. The {@condition charmed} target regards Strahd as a trusted friend to be heeded and protected, by force if needed. The target isn’t under Strahd’s control, but it takes Strahd’s request and actions in the most favorable way and lets Strahd bite it. Each time Strahd or his companions do anything harmful to the target, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. Otherwise the effect lasts 24 hours or until Strahd is destroyed, in on a different plane of existence than the target, or takes a bonus action to end the effect.

action.name: Blood Drain (Mist Form Only)
entries: One creature in the vampire’s space must make a {@dc 22} Constitution saving throw (undead and constructs automatically succeed). On a failed save, the target takes 22 ({@damage 4d6 + 8}) necrotic damage, its hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken, and the vampire regains hit points equal to that amount. This reduction to the target’s hit point maximum lasts until the target finishes a long rest. It dies if this effect reduces its hit point maximum to 0.

action.name: Feral Swarm {@recharge 5}
entries: A giant swarm of bats gather around the higher vampire and descend upon a 90-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 22} Dexterity saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone} and take {@damage 4d6} bludgeoning damage plus {@damage 4d4} piercing damage. On a successful save, the creature takes only half the damage and isn’t knocked {@condition prone}.

action.name: Children of the Night
entries: Strahd magically calls {@dice 2d4} swarms of bats or swarms of rats, provided that the sun isn’t up. While outdoors, Strand can call {@dice 3d6} wolves instead. The called creatures arrive in {@dice 1d4} rounds, acting as allies of Strand and obeying his spoken commands. The beasts remain for 1 hour, until Strand dies, or until he dismisses them as a bonus action.

bonus.name: Bite
entries: Strahd can use a bonus action to make a bite attack. Melee Weapon Attack: +{@hit 14} to hit, reach 5ft., one willing creature, or a creature that is {@condition grappled} by Strahd,{@condition incapacitated}, or {@condition restrained}. Hit 18 ({@damage 3d6 + 8}) piercing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) necrotic damage. The target’s hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken, and Strahd regains hit points equal to that amount. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if its hit point maximum is reduced to 0. A humanoid slain in this way and then buried in the ground rises the following night as a vampire spawn under Strand’s control.

bonus.name: Beguiling gaze
entries: As a bonus action, Strahd fixes his gaze on a creature he can see within 30 feet of him. If the target can see Strahd, the target must succeed on a {@dc 22} Wisdom saving throw or Strahd has advantage on all attack rolls against that target. The effect lasts until the target takes damage or until the start of Strahd’s next turn. For that time, the affected creature is also a willing target for Strahd’s bite attack. A creature that can’t be {@condition charmed} is immune to this effect. A creature that successfully saves against Strahd’s gaze is immune to it for 1 hour.

bonus.name: Summon equipment
entries: Strahd magically summons his armor and sword. The armor and sword magically replaces the armor, cloths and weapon that Strahd was wearing.

reaction.name: Counterspell(3/long rest)
entries: Strahd can use a reaction to cast counterspell.

legendaryHeader: Strahd can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. If Strahd has unspent legendary actions at the end of the round, it may spend and immediately use its remaining legendary actions. Strahd regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Attack. (Cost 1 Action)
entries: Strahd makes a longsword or unarmed strike attack.

legendary.name: Move (Cost 1 Action)
entries: Strahd can move up to his speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Shocking Bolt (Vampire form only, costs 1 action)
entries: Strahd’s Armor attacks an enemy. Ranged Spell Attack: +{@hit 4} to hit (with advantage on the attack roll if the target is wearing armor made of metal), range 60 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 3d6}) lightning damage.

legendary.name: Frightening Gaze (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Strahd fixes its gaze on one creature it can see within 10 feet of it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 22} WIS saving throw against this magic or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute. The {@condition frightened} target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a target’s saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the target is immune to this effect for the next 24 hours.

legendary.name: Ancient Might (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The vampire makes one attack with its claws. If it hits, the attack is a critical hit. A creature taking damage from the attack suffers from a Lingering Injury (See p. 272 of the Dungeon Master’s Guide).

legendary.name: Shadow Mirror (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Strahd teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying. Alternatively, it can choose a space within range that is occupied by a Small or Medium creature. If that creature is unwilling, it must make on a {@dc 22} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, both creatures teleport, swapping places.

legendary.name: Charm (Cost 2 Actions)
entries: Strahd makes a charm attack.
{@link The Real Devil Strahd|https://www.dmsguild.com/product/226077/The-Real-Devil-Strahd--A-CR27-Version-of-the-Devil}
SHORT DESCRIPTION

This document contains a CR 27 version of Lord Strahd Von Zarovich. I felt that the book version was pretty weak compared to the novels and descriptions of Strahd. According to the lore, he is the first true vampire and the one who defeated mordekainen, while the CR 15 version that the “Curse of Strahd” book uses; is similar to a normal vampire who could have never managed to defeat a wizard like morderkainen. This version is made to be used only at the final encounter, it is quite deadly and will most likely result in a total party kill if used incorrectly.
WHAT IS DIFFERENT?

- The Fanes In this version, Strahd is being empowered by the three Fanes of wilderness. You can find more information about the fanes on the “Expedition to castle Ravenloft 3.5” module. If you like you can just remove the fanes completly,by ignoring the benefits listed in the stat block and avoiding the sidequests to destroy them. New legendary and lair actions New legendary and lair actions have been added to the ones of the original statblock. They give environmental,flavour and mechanical benefits worthy only to the Lord of Castle Ravenloft. The heart’s of sorrow bonus life points have been doubled to make the shielding effect to feel more dreadful.

- New Spell list and new spells The spell list of Strahd has been adjusted to the power of someone who has access to the amber temple. I have made his list combat ready. The spells that you can find in the spell list of the book are pretty useless to a final showdown. You can also find new spells of previous editions translated to 5th ed. at the end of this file.

- Equipment As the lord of the land, Its totaly unacceptable for strahd to not have magic items equiped when he fights to kill his enemies. I have equipped him with his animated armor, a +3 longsword, a +3 ring of protection, a ring of counterspell and other magical items that can be found in the statblock.
- MONSTROUS FORM! Strahd will transform to the real “devil Strahd” if you anger him enough ;)

entries.name: Strahd carries the following magic items:
entries: - 1 Ring of {@spell counterspell|PHB|Counterspell} (3 charges/dawn)
- 1 {@item Blacksteel longsword|p4535992Items|Blacksteel longsword +3}
- 2 {@item Strahd's animated armor|p4535992Items|Animated Armor of Strahd +2}
- 1 {@item Bead of Force|DMG}
